sections.heading: Systemic
sections.content:
Systemic therapy is indicated for patients with advanced/metastatic HCC (HCC-6) or for progression on/after locoregional therapy (HCC-5). Treatment selection is based on Child-Pugh class, performance status, prior therapy, and biomarker status. Most pivotal trials were in CTP A patients; use in CTP B requires caution and extrapolation [HCC-I, HCC-E].

First-Line, Unresectable HCC, CTP A: Preferred regimen: Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab (Category 1) [HCC-I]. Rationale: IMbrave150 trial showed improved OS and PFS vs. sorafenib [HCC-I, MS-33]. Alternatives: Durvalumab + Tremelimumab-actl (Category 1), Ipilimumab + Nivolumab (Category 1, with counseling on early mortality risk) [HCC-I]. Biomarker requirements: No specific biomarker required for these regimens. Endoscopic evaluation/management for varices required before atezolizumab + bevacizumab [HCC-I].

First-Line, Unresectable HCC, CTP A (Other Recommended): Preferred regimen: Durvalumab (Cat 1), Lenvatinib (Cat 1), Sorafenib (Cat 1), Tislelizumab-jsgr (Cat 1), Pembrolizumab (Cat 2B) [HCC-I]. Rationale: Non-inferior or superior data to sorafenib in phase 3 trials [HCC-I]. Alternatives: [none]. Biomarker requirements: Pembrolizumab is recommended regardless of MSI status; FDA-approved for MSI-H tumors in subsequent-line [HCC-I].

Subsequent-Line, after progression on prior systemic therapy: Preferred regimen: Cabozantinib (Category 1), Regorafenib (Category 1) [HCC-I]. Rationale: CELESTIAL and RESORCE trials showed improved OS/PFS vs. placebo after sorafenib progression [HCC-I, MS-36]. Alternatives: First-line regimens not previously used (e.g., atezolizumab + bevacizumab if no prior checkpoint inhibitor) [HCC-I]. Biomarker requirements: None for these general options.

Subsequent-Line, Specific Biomarker-Driven: Preferred regimen: Ramucirumab (if AFP ≥400 ng/mL, Cat 1), Dostarlimab-gxly (for MSI-H/dMMR, Cat 2B), Selpercatinib (for RET fusion, Cat 2B), Entrectinib/Larotrectinib/Repotrectinib (for NTRK fusions) [HCC-I]. Rationale: Biomarker-specific efficacy demonstrated in trials [HCC-I]. Alternatives: [none]. Biomarker requirements: AFP ≥400 ng/mL for ramucirumab. MSI-H/dMMR for dostarlimab. RET gene fusion for selpercatinib. NTRK1/2/3 gene fusion for entrectinib/larotrectinib/repotrectinib [HCC-I].

cards.name: Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab
cards.drugs: Atezolizumab 1200 mg IV every 3 weeks, Bevacizumab 15 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks
cards.detail: Setting: First-line, unresectable HCC, CTP A. Cycle days: 21. Key toxicities: Hypertension, proteinuria, diarrhea, fatigue, immune-mediated adverse events. Requires pre-treatment endoscopy for varices [HCC-I, MS-33].

cards.name: Durvalumab + Tremelimumab-actl (STRIDE regimen)
cards.drugs: Tremelimumab-actl 300 mg IV single dose, Durvalumab 1500 mg IV starting 30 days after tremelimumab, then every 4 weeks
cards.detail: Setting: First-line, unresectable HCC, CTP A. Cycle days: 28 (after initial tremelimumab dose). Key toxicities: Immune-mediated adverse events, diarrhea/colitis, hepatitis [HCC-I, MS-34].

cards.name: Ipilimumab + Nivolumab
cards.drugs: Ipilimumab 1 mg/kg IV every 3 weeks x4 doses, Nivolumab 240 mg IV every 2 weeks (starting with first ipilimumab dose)
cards.detail: Setting: First-line, unresectable HCC, CTP A. Cycle days: 21 for ipilimumab induction, then 14 for nivolumab. Key toxicities: Immune-mediated adverse events; higher rate of early death (first 6 months) and high-dose steroid use (29%) per CheckMate-9DW trial. Counsel patients accordingly [HCC-I, MS-35].

cards.name: Cabozantinib
cards.detail: Setting: Subsequent-line, HCC after progression on sorafenib, CTP A. Cycle days: 28 (continuous dosing). Key toxicities: Hypertension, diarrhea, palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia, weight loss, fatigue [HCC-I, MS-36].

cards.name: Regorafenib
cards.drugs: Regorafenib 160 mg oral daily, first 3 weeks of 4-week cycle
cards.detail: Setting: Subsequent-line, HCC after progression on sorafenib, CTP A. Cycle days: 28 (3 weeks on, 1 week off). Key toxicities: Hand-foot skin reaction, diarrhea, hypertension, fatigue [HCC-I, MS-36].

cards.name: Ramucirumab
cards.detail: Setting: Subsequent-line, HCC with AFP ≥400 ng/mL after progression on sorafenib. Cycle days: 14. Key toxicities: Hypertension, proteinuria, diarrhea [HCC-I].

timeline.title: 1st Line
timeline.body: Atezolizumab + Bevacizumab, Durvalumab + Tremelimumab-actl, Ipilimumab + Nivolumab, Durvalumab, Lenvatinib, Sorafenib, Tislelizumab-jsgr, Pembrolizumab

timeline.title: Subsequent-Line
timeline.body: Cabozantinib, Regorafenib, Ramucirumab (if AFP ≥400), Dostarlimab-gxly (MSI-H/dMMR), Selpercatinib (RET fusion), Entrectinib/Larotrectinib/Repotrectinib (NTRK fusions), plus first-line regimens not previously used
